title: Compose (v2 authoring kind)
description:
The `compose` operator — authoring surface for user-defined derivations. Lets operators declare new kinds as compositions of the 6 primitives (or extensions of stdlib kinds). See docs/compose-from-primitives.md for the authoring guide.

Policy slot authoring: `policy_type` is a FREE-FORM STRING — you pick a value that names the policy's domain (e.g. 'legal_constraint', 'escalation_rules', 'treatment_rules', 'regulatory_screening'). It is not an enum. Put the policy's concrete rules under `statement`, `applies_to` lists the scope_refs the policy governs, and `exceptions` lists scopes where it doesn't apply. Example: { primitive: 'Policy', role: 'escalation', fields: { policy_type: 'escalation_rules', statement: {...}, applies_to: ['support.tier1'] } }.

Entity slots hold attributes keyed by your domain names. Event slots carry `event_type` (free-form, e.g. 'result_recorded'). Relation slots carry `relation_type` ('cites', 'belongs_to', 'measures') + `source_entity_id` + `target_entity_id`.

name.description: The composed kind's name. Capital-first, no underscores (e.g. 'ContractClause', 'CodeReviewSession').

namespace.description: Dotted namespace for the composed kind (e.g. 'legal.acme'). Prevents cross-branch name collisions.

compose.description: The composition expression — a list of primitive or stdlib-extension slots. Slots merge into a single kind. Field collisions require !override on one side.

interface.description: Declared interface for this kind — fields exposed to other composables. Used by interface-check at weave lint time.
